# Terraform MongoDB Atlas Cluster with AWS Integration

 [![Latest Release](https://img.shields.io/github/v/release/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-mongoatlas-aws-cluster.svg?style=for-the-badge)](https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-mongoatlas-aws-cluster/releases/latest) [![Last Updated](https://img.shields.io/github/last-commit/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-mongoatlas-aws-cluster.svg?style=for-the-badge)](https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-mongoatlas-aws-cluster/commits)


Terraform module for provisioning MongoDB Atlas clusters with AWS provider integration.
Wraps the generic [`terraform-module-mongoatlas-cluster`](https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-mongoatlas-cluster)
and adds AWS-specific features: Secrets Manager credential storage, KMS encryption,
Lambda-based password rotation, and HOOP connection management.


---

This project is part of our comprehensive approach towards DevOps Acceleration. 
[<img align="right" title="Share via Email" width="24" height="24" src="https://docs.cloudops.works/images/ionicons/ios-mail.svg"/>][share_email]

It's 100% Open Source and licensed under the [APACHE2](LICENSE).







We have [*lots of terraform modules*][terraform_modules] that are Open Source and we are trying to get them well-maintained!. Check them out!






## Introduction

This module is the AWS-specific wrapper for the generic MongoDB Atlas cluster module.
It handles all AWS provider interactions, including:

- MongoDB Atlas cluster provisioning (delegated to the generic module)
- AWS Secrets Manager for secure admin credential storage
- Optional AWS KMS encryption for the secret
- Optional AWS Lambda-based password rotation via Secrets Manager rotation
- HOOP connection configuration referencing the Secrets Manager secret
- Automatic region detection via `data.aws_region.current`

The cluster admin credentials are exposed both via the `cluster_secrets_credentials`
output (the Secrets Manager secret name) and are stored in JSON format inside the secret.

## Usage


**IMPORTANT:** The `master` branch is used in `source` just as an example. In your code, do not pin to `master` because there may be breaking changes between releases.
Instead pin to the release tag (e.g. `?ref=vX.Y.Z`) of one of our [latest releases](https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-mongoatlas-aws-cluster/releases).


### Terragrunt Usage

```hcl
# terragrunt.hcl
include "root" {
  path = find_in_parent_folders()
}

terraform {
  source = "git::https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-mongoatlas-aws-cluster.git?ref=v1.2.1"
}

inputs = {
  project_id  = "your-atlas-project-id"
  name        = "my-cluster"

  settings = {
    cluster_type:  "REPLICASET"      # (Optional) REPLICASET | SHARDED | GEOSHARDED. Default: "REPLICASET"
    major_version: 7.0               # (Optional) MongoDB major version. Default: null
    termination_protection: true     # (Optional) Enable termination protection. Default: null
    version_release: "LTS"           # (Optional) LTS | CONTINUOUS. Default: "LTS"

    admin_user:
      enabled:                  true     # (Optional) Create Atlas admin user. Default: false
      username:                 "admin"  # (Optional) Username. Default: auto-generated
      auth_database:            "admin"  # (Optional) Authentication database. Default: "admin"
      use_external_rotation:    false    # (Optional) When true, an external rotation manager (e.g. Lambda) handles the password. Default: false
      kms_key_id:               ""       # (Optional) AWS KMS key ARN/Alias for Secrets Manager encryption
      rotation_lambda_name:     ""       # (Optional) Lambda function name for Secrets Manager rotation. Required when use_external_rotation is true.
      rotation_period:          90       # (Optional) Lambda rotation period in days. Default: 90
      rotation_duration:        "1h"     # (Optional) Lambda rotation duration. Default: "1h"
      rotate_immediately:       true     # (Optional) Rotate secret on first creation (AWS Secrets Manager). Default: true
      password_rotation_period: 90       # (Optional) Terraform-managed rotation period in days. Default: 90

    regions:
      - provider:          "AWS"        # (Optional) AWS | GCP | AZURE | TENANT. Default: "TENANT"
        backing_provider:  "AWS"        # (Optional) Backing provider. Default: null
        region:            "US_EAST_1"  # (Optional) Atlas region. Default: from AWS region
        priority:          7            # (Optional) Node priority 1-7. Default: 7
        electable:
          size:  "M10"    # (Optional) Instance size. Default: "M2"
          count: 3        # (Optional) Node count. Default: null

    backup:
      enabled:             true  # (Optional) Enable cloud backup. Default: false
      hour_of_day:         2     # (Optional) Backup hour 0-23. Default: 0
      restore_window_days: 7     # (Optional) Restore window in days. Default: 1
      daily:
        retention_value: 7       # (Optional) Daily retention in days. Default: 7
      weekly:
        retention_value: 4       # (Optional) Weekly retention in weeks. Default: 4
      monthly:
        retention_value: 12      # (Optional) Monthly retention in months. Default: 12

    hoop:
      enabled: false             # (Optional) Configure HOOP connection. Default: false
      agent:   "hoop-agent"      # (Required when enabled) HOOP agent name
      tags:    []                # (Optional) Additional HOOP tags. Default: []
  }

  run_hoop = false  # (Optional) Execute HOOP CLI command via null_resource. Default: false
}
```

## Quick Start

1. Configure AWS and MongoDB Atlas credentials:
   ```bash
   export AWS_REGION="us-east-1"
   export MONGODB_ATLAS_PUBLIC_KEY="your_public_key"
   export MONGODB_ATLAS_PRIVATE_KEY="your_private_key"
   ```

2. Create a Terragrunt configuration using the usage example above.

3. Initialize and apply:
   ```bash
   terraform init && terraform apply
   ```

4. Retrieve credentials from Secrets Manager:
   ```bash
   aws secretsmanager get-secret-value \
     --secret-id $(terraform output -raw cluster_secrets_credentials) \
     --query SecretString --output text | jq .
   ```


## Examples

### Basic Production Cluster

```hcl
inputs = {
  project_id = "your-atlas-project-id"
  name       = "prod-cluster"

  settings = {
    cluster_type           = "REPLICASET"
    major_version          = 7.0
    termination_protection = true
    admin_user = {
      enabled  = true
      username = "prod-admin"
    }
    regions = [{
      provider         = "AWS"
      backing_provider = "AWS"
      region           = "US_EAST_1"
      priority         = 7
      electable = {
        size  = "M30"
        count = 3
      }
    }]
  }
}
```

### Cluster with Lambda Password Rotation

```hcl
inputs = {
  project_id = "your-atlas-project-id"
  name       = "secure-cluster"

  settings = {
    admin_user = {
      enabled              = true
      kms_key_id           = "alias/my-kms-key"
      rotation_lambda_name = "atlas-password-rotation"
      rotation_period      = 30
      rotation_duration    = "1h"
      rotate_immediately   = true
    }
    regions = [{ ... }]
  }
}
```

### Cluster with HOOP Integration

```hcl
inputs = {
  project_id = "your-atlas-project-id"
  name       = "hoop-cluster"
  run_hoop   = true

  settings = {
    admin_user = { enabled = true }
    hoop = {
      enabled = true
      agent   = "my-hoop-agent"
      tags    = ["env:production", "team:platform"]
    }
    regions = [{ ... }]
  }
}
```

## Inputs

| Name | Description | Type | Default | Required |
|------|-------------|------|---------|:--------:|
| <a name="input_extra_tags"></a> [extra\_tags](#input\_extra\_tags) | Extra tags to add to the resources | `map(string)` | `{}` | no |
| <a name="input_is_hub"></a> [is\_hub](#input\_is\_hub) | Is this a hub or spoke configuration? | `bool` | `false` | no |
| <a name="input_name"></a> [name](#input\_name) | Name of the resource | `string` | `""` | no |
| <a name="input_name_prefix"></a> [name\_prefix](#input\_name\_prefix) | Prefix for the name of the resources | `string` | `""` | no |
| <a name="input_org"></a> [org](#input\_org) | Organization details | <pre>object({<br/>    organization_name = string<br/>    organization_unit = string<br/>    environment_type  = string<br/>    environment_name  = string<br/>  })</pre> | n/a | yes |
| <a name="input_project_id"></a> [project\_id](#input\_project\_id) | (optional) The ID of the project where the cluster will be created | `string` | `""` | no |
| <a name="input_project_name"></a> [project\_name](#input\_project\_name) | (optional) The name of the project where the cluster will be created | `string` | `""` | no |
| <a name="input_run_hoop"></a> [run\_hoop](#input\_run\_hoop) | Run hoop with agent via null\_resource local-exec. Requires HOOP CLI to be installed and authenticated on the Terraform runner. | `bool` | `false` | no |
| <a name="input_settings"></a> [settings](#input\_settings) | Settings for the MongoDB Atlas cluster and AWS integrations | `any` | `{}` | no |
| <a name="input_spoke_def"></a> [spoke\_def](#input\_spoke\_def) | Spoke ID Number, must be a 3 digit number | `string` | `"001"` | no |
